narrative: Dear [Student's Name], as a Black woman applying to law school, we know how much heart and determination it takes to pursue this path, and we're here to help you understand the automated decision on your application in a clear way. Your profile includes Year 1 and Year 3 grades placing you in the second decile at your school—meaning you ranked toward the lower end compared to your peers—along with an undergraduate GPA of 3.00, an LSAT score of 32, middle family income, and plans to attend full-time, which is the norm for most students. The model assessed your likelihood of failing the bar exam on the first try at 68.9%, a high enough risk that it led to the rejection of your admission to protect the school's outcomes. The biggest factors pushing toward this failure prediction were your Year 3 decile ranking, which had the strongest negative influence by signaling lower relative performance compared to typical students who rank around the fifth or sixth decile on average. Your Year 1 decile followed closely as the second-most influential, again highlighting early academic standing below the dataset average of about the sixth decile. On a more positive note, your undergraduate GPA pulled somewhat in the direction of passing, as it's close to the average of 3.21, though not enough to offset the others. Your middle family income contributed moderately toward the failure prediction, differing from higher averages in the data, while your LSAT score offered a small boost toward success despite being below the typical 36.7, and full-time status had minimal impact since over 90% of students share that. Overall, your academic deciles stood out as unusually low against peers who succeed, tipping the scales despite some strengths. This isn't a judgment on your potential—many overcome similar starts—but the model prioritizes bar passage patterns from past data. Next, consider retaking the LSAT to aim higher, strengthening your recent grades through targeted courses, or exploring part-time programs or gap-year prep to build a stronger profile. We're rooting for you; with focused improvements, you can reapply stronger and show the world what you're capable of. Keep pushing forward.
